description: I was pishing at willow clump, when a towhee flew away from me and tee'd up on a willow branch, fully in view. Initially, the view was almost strict profile; before the bird flew, it faced away, leaning a bit forward and cocking its tail up, giving me a good view of vent and undertail. The head, upper chest, and upperparts were an odd brown, not dissimilar to milk chocolate but with a warmer tone and a bit darker (I guess it partly depends on the brand of milk chocolate). In any case, a color that I've seen repeatedly in female Eastern Towhees. The wings were utterly plain (including tertials), except a square of white at the base of the primaries with a narrow white line extending down lower edge of folded primaries. The bill was relatively short c/w that of a Spotted Towhee and the head more rounded. The underparts below chest were white with orange-buff sides. The undertail coverts were a paler more yellow-buff. The tail had white "spots" that extended proximally almost to the tip of the undertail coverts.
